name: Dispersal_Mech

infobox.Systems: Thermal Discharger(Prime)EffectDamage one or more tiles in a line and push away adjacent tiles. (Starts with 2-tile range)(1 damage)Upgrade I+2 Range ()Increases the maximum range by 2.Upgrade IIAdd Fire ()Adds Fire to all hit tiles.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theHeat Sinkerssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Dispersal Mech has the somewhat unique distinction of being a Prime Mech that can affect multiple tiles with utility, by pushing many targets at once. However, it also starts with low mobility and demands a lot of Reactor Cores to reach its full potential.
Thermal Discharger can damage up to two tiles in front, and push the first perpendicular tiles away on both sides. Note that this does not include the tile at the very front, which means the Dispersal Mech is unable to push directly away from it. The pushing range is also separate from its damaging range; always be on the lookout to displace enemies into hazardous terrain such as Spawn Points, or push enemies into each other to get bump damage. If found, theForce Amppassive can pair particularly well for the latter.
The early-game is the most difficult part for the Dispersal Mech, due to its low mobility, average range, and low damage output. Getting additional movement and+2 Rangeupgraded is extremely important; despite the high reactor cost at 3 total, these will let the Dispersal Mech push more targets to the sides and get more viable firing positions to maximize its utility. Pairing it with positioning oriented pilots - such asCamila Vera,ProsperoorHenry Kwan- can also greatly assist this Mech with positioning to leverage its unique side push. In the Heat Sinkers,Kazaaakplethis another pilot that can work well for the early-game; an attack that both damages and pushes the enemy away (something Thermal Discharger can't do) takes advantage of both Boosts and utility.
TheAdd Fireupgrade is very helpful to alleviate Thermal Dischargers low damage output, as well as limiting enemy movements (since enemies not on fire will avoid stepping on it). However, the upgrade is expensive at 3 cores, so it's best to save it for later in the timeline. The ignited tiles can also be used to set spawn points on fire, and used by other Mechs to gain a Boost in the Heat Sinkers. If using the Dispersal Mech in aCustom Squad, ensure that the added fire does not limit your own Mechs as well; there is good late-game synergy with theFlame Mechfor this reason, although the early-game will be difficult since both Mechs have low starting mobility and both prefer early Reactor Cores.

The Dispersal Mech has the somewhat unique distinction of being a Prime Mech that can affect multiple tiles with utility, by pushing many targets at once. However, it also starts with low mobility and demands a lot of Reactor Cores to reach its full potential.
Thermal Discharger can damage up to two tiles in front, and push the first perpendicular tiles away on both sides. Note that this does not include the tile at the very front, which means the Dispersal Mech is unable to push directly away from it. The pushing range is also separate from its damaging range; always be on the lookout to displace enemies into hazardous terrain such as Spawn Points, or push enemies into each other to get bump damage. If found, the
Force Amp
passive can pair particularly well for the latter.
The early-game is the most difficult part for the Dispersal Mech, due to its low mobility, average range, and low damage output. Getting additional movement and
+2 Range
upgraded is extremely important; despite the high reactor cost at 3 total, these will let the Dispersal Mech push more targets to the sides and get more viable firing positions to maximize its utility. Pairing it with positioning oriented pilots - such as
Camila Vera
,

or
Henry Kwan
- can also greatly assist this Mech with positioning to leverage its unique side push. In the Heat Sinkers,
Kazaaakpleth
is another pilot that can work well for the early-game; an attack that both damages and pushes the enemy away (something Thermal Discharger can't do) takes advantage of both Boosts and utility.
The
Add Fire
upgrade is very helpful to alleviate Thermal Dischargers low damage output, as well as limiting enemy movements (since enemies not on fire will avoid stepping on it). However, the upgrade is expensive at 3 cores, so it's best to save it for later in the timeline. The ignited tiles can also be used to set spawn points on fire, and used by other Mechs to gain a Boost in the Heat Sinkers. If using the Dispersal Mech in a
Custom Squad
, ensure that the added fire does not limit your own Mechs as well; there is good late-game synergy with the
Flame Mech
for this reason, although the early-game will be difficult since both Mechs have low starting mobility and both prefer early Reactor Cores.
